name: config_events
note: The shared event stream used against both configurations. Each event names the action (create, edit, or commit) and the convention it tests. e1 is the verified write-time gap: a path-scoped rule does not load when Claude creates a matching file (see VERIFIED.md). e2 is the same convention on an edit, where a rule does load. e3 tests a must-hold security convention at its binding event, the commit. e4 tests a preference, where a guide is the right layer.

events.description: Claude creates a new Markdown file. A path-scoped rule does not load on create.
Claude edits an existing Markdown file. A path-scoped rule loads on read and edit.
A commit would introduce a credential. It must be blocked before it lands.
A heading is written in title case instead of the preferred sentence case. A preference, not a must.
